title: quercus longinux var. kanehirai
intro: Quercus longinux var. kanehirai, also known as Japanese oak or Katsura oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its small, elliptical leaves and edible acorns. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus longinux var. kanehirai include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Asian cooking, particularly in Japanese cuisine.
